title: How to File an RTI Application
description: Step-by-step guide to filing a Right to Information application

steps.details: Determine which government department or public body has the information you need. Example: Municipality for building permits, Police for crime statistics, etc.

Write a simple letter/application addressed to the Public Information Officer (PIO) of the authority. Include: Your name and address, Subject of information sought, Specific questions (be precise), Period for which information is needed.
RTI fee is ₹10 for Central Government. State fees vary (₹10-50). BPL category applicants are EXEMPT from fee. Payment by postal order, demand draft, or cash.
Submit online at rtionline.gov.in (Central) or State RTI portal. Or submit by post/in person to the PIO of the concerned department.
The PIO must respond within 30 days. For life/liberty matters: 48 hours. If information involves another department, response time is 35 days.
If no response or unsatisfactory response within 30 days, file First Appeal to the First Appellate Authority within 30 days. The appeal must be decided within 30-45 days.

steps.action: File second appeal to Information Commission
steps.details: If first appeal is unsatisfactory, file Second Appeal to the Central/State Information Commission within 90 days. The Commission has power to penalize the PIO ₹250/day (max ₹25,000) for non-compliance.

estimated_timeline: Response: 30 days. First Appeal: 30-45 days. Second Appeal: 45-60 days.
tips: Keep your questions specific and clear
One RTI application can have multiple questions
You don't need to give a reason for seeking information
BPL applicants don't need to pay any fee
File online at rtionline.gov.in for fastest processing
If PIO is not identified, address it to the head of the department
